ID LIKE TO MAKE A PRETTY TO LOOK AT SLASH INTERACTIVE VERSION OF THE PARENTING AGREEEMENT IJ AMG OING TO BE FILING WITH THE COURT - HERE SHE BE - THANKS DADDIO




1. THE "HARPER BASELINE"
This Parenting Agreement rejects the standard adversarial model. Instead, it establishes the "Harper Baseline": a verified standard where safety is proven by data ("The Exhibit Generator"), not by hearsay. It prioritizes Harper's right to a stable, sober father and an autonomous mother—both protected, both free.
2. NOTICE OF URGENT FACTUAL CLARIFICATION (PSR REBUTTAL)
The Applicant alerts the Court that the Pre-Sentence Report currently on file contains demonstrable fabrications that threaten the integrity of this proceeding:
The "Social Drinker" Fallacy: The report labels the Father a "social drinker." This is dangerous and false. The Father is 370+ Days Sober and committed to Total Abstinence.
The "40-Minutes Late" Fabrication: The report claims the Father was late for intake. Witness Testimony from the front desk receptionist confirms the Father was on time.
The "Ghost Calls": Telecom logs prove the Father was not evasive; he was waiting.
3. THE "AUTONOMY" INVITATION
This document is an invitation to the Mother, Emma Ryan. It acknowledges her strengths and current probationary status, and provides the legal structure she needs to assert Parental Primacy—ensuring that decisions are made by Mom and Dad, not by extended family pressure ("The Circle of Two").
4. THE CONCLUSION
"We, the parents, haven't been told 'No' when we stand together. This document is the architecture of that unity."
DEDICATION: THE LESSON OF THE JOURNEY
"This legal battle has been a classroom, and the lesson is clear:
Harper needs her parents as a unified team.
Apart, we found we had no control over the outcome. We were at the mercy of reports, lawyers, and strangers.
But when we look at the history of this family, we realize a fundamental truth:
As a team, the three of us have never been told 'No.'
We survived the trauma. We survived the separation. We survived the noise.
This Agreement creates the structure for that Team.
It is a promise to finally stop facing off, and start standing Back-to-Back.
To always watch the other's blind spot.
To always protect the family unit.
To have each other's backs—just as Dad has had ours, even in the silence."
TABLE OF CONTENTS
PART ONE: THE FOUNDATION
Preamble & Core Values
The "Harper Baseline" & Verification System
The "Circle of Two" Doctrine
PART TWO: PHYSICAL CUSTODY & ACCESS
Custody Framework & Shared Parenting Model
The Three-Phase Model (Months 1-36+)
Access Schedules & Advancement Criteria
PART THREE: SAFETY PROTOCOLS & THE "EXHIBIT GENERATOR"
Substance Use Monitoring & Zero Tolerance
The "Red Line" Triggers & Immediate Suspension
The "Glass House" & Monthly Verification Reports
Medical Safety & Lethality Protocols
PART FOUR: DECISION-MAKING & PARENTAL AUTHORITY
Medical Authority (Father—Sole)
Educational & Lifestyle Authority (Mother—Lead)
The "Circle of Two" & Exclusion of Extended Family
Joint Decisions & Resolution Process
PART FIVE: DEVELOPMENTAL & BEHAVIORAL SUPPORT
Parenting Education & Skills Building (Joint Participation)
Mental Health & Counseling Support
The "Bear Protocol" (Anti-Alienation Protections)
Communication Standards & The "Family Table"
PART SIX: THE "RIGHT OF FIRST REFUSAL" & AUTONOMY SUPPORT
The "Right of First Refusal" Protocol
The "Jane Block" (Autonomy Protection)
The "Support & Strength" Pledge
PART SEVEN: DISPUTE RESOLUTION & ENFORCEMENT
The Parenting Coordinator & Day-to-Day Resolution
The "Ombudsman" Right
Modification Process & Material Changes
Contempt Remedies & Legal Enforcement
PART EIGHT: LEGACY & TRANSFORMATION CLAUSES
The "God and Fatherhood" Transformation
The Sunrise Clause (March 1, 2028) & Cost Elimination
Letters to Harper & Emma
The Funeral & Graduation Protocol
The Architects' Pledge
APPENDICES
A. Parenting Education Programs
B. Substance Screening Protocols & Facilities
C. Therapist Information & Consent Forms
D. Communication Platform Standards
E. Pre-Sentence Report Factual Corrections
F. Exhibit Generator Sample Report
G. Crisis Contact Information
PART ONE: THE FOUNDATION
MODULE 1: PREAMBLE & CORE VALUES
1.1 The Integrity of Recovery
The Father operates under the spirit of Proverbs 28:13: "Whoever conceals their sins does not prosper, but the one who confesses and renounces them finds mercy."
He explicitly rejects the minimization of his past in the PSR and asks the Court to take Judicial Notice of his "Benchmark of Stability":
370+ Days Sober (as of January 24, 2026)
2 Years Gambling-Free
Successful navigation of a 129-day administrative separation without breach of peace
Completion of Celebrate Recovery (spiritual foundation)
Establishment of Kings Church community (ongoing accountability)
Secure housing and stable employment
Demonstrated commitment to fatherhood
This is not minimization. This is transformation.
1.2 The Mother's Position in This Framework
The Mother, Emma Elizabeth Ryan, is recognized as:
A capable, loving parent who deserves independence and autonomy
A woman operating under probationary status who is committed to demonstrated compliance
A person whose judgment has been systematically undermined by her family of origin
A mother whose highest and best use is to parent Harper without approval-seeking from her parents
This agreement is explicitly designed to liberate Emma from external family control and position her as an equal co-architect of Harper's future. It acknowledges her willingness to engage in the programs and supports necessary for sustainable recovery and independent parenting.
1.3 Harper's Perspective
Harper Catherine Ryan-Schulz has the right to:
Know both her parents as whole, capable humans
Benefit from her father's recovery and transformation
Have a mother who is free to parent without external interference
Experience her parents as a unified team, not as warring camps
Live in two homes, but experience one unified family system
MODULE 2: THE "HARPER BASELINE" & VERIFICATION SYSTEM
2.1 The Problem Being Addressed
What exists now:
Unverified claims about sobriety, stability, and parenting capacity
Reports filled with hearsay, assumption, and bias
A system where accusations can be made without evidence
A mother afraid to assert her authority without external validation
A father whose transformation is minimized or disbelieved
What this plan creates:
Objective, measurable data about safety and stability
A "Glass House" where both parents' commitment is visible and verifiable
A system where claims are backed by evidence, not emotion
A mother empowered to make her own decisions within clear parameters
A father whose redemption is acknowledged and measured
2.2 The "Exhibit Generator" (Monthly Verification Report)
Beginning immediately, the Father will generate a monthly "Exhibit Generator" report documenting:
Bio-Medical Results:
Substance screening results (13-panel drug/alcohol screen)
Date, time, facility, results
All testing performed per Section 7.2 protocols
Meeting Logs:
Celebrate Recovery attendance (weekly)
Kings Church attendance (minimum 2x monthly)
Therapy/counseling sessions (as scheduled)
Any additional support group attendance
Behavioral Compliance:
No police contact
Employment status and stability
Housing stability confirmation
Transportation/vehicle registration current
Report Format:
Auto-generated monthly (by the 3rd of each month)
Sent to: [Parenting Coordinator] + [Mother's lawyer] + [Father's copy]
Confidential but verifiable
No subjective narrative—only objective data
Purpose: This removes Emma from the role of "policeman" and creates a neutral, third-party verification system. Emma can see that Craig is meeting his commitments without having to monitor him herself.
2.3 The "Glass House" Principle
The Father agrees to "Hire the System." He waives privacy rights for:
All substance screening results
Attendance records at support groups and churches
Therapist confirmation of engagement (with appropriate privacy boundaries)
Employment verification
This is not punishment. It is transparency that builds trust.
The Logic: If you have nothing to hide, hiding nothing proves you have nothing to hide.
MODULE 3: THE "CIRCLE OF TWO" DOCTRINE
3.1 Parental Primacy
The authority to raise Harper belongs exclusively and equally to:
Craig Alexander Paul Schulz (Father)
Emma Elizabeth Ryan (Mother)
Extended family—grandmothers, aunts, uncles, family friends—are loved guests in Harper's life. They have zero vote in parenting decisions.
3.2 The Chain of Command
Who decides what Harper wears, where she goes to school, what church (if any) she attends, what doctor she sees?
Answer: Mom and Dad. Not Grandma. Not extended family. Mom and Dad.
If there is a conflict between parents and extended family, the conflict is resolved between parents. Extended family does not get a seat at the table.
3.3 Breaking the Cycle of Enmeshment
The Mother's family of origin (specifically Jane Ryan and Sonny Ryan) have operated as de facto co-parents, overriding Emma's decisions and creating competing authority structures.
This agreement explicitly breaks that pattern.
Emma is granted:
Sole authority over lifestyle decisions (what Harper wears, haircuts, extracurricular choices)
Lead authority over educational decisions (school selection, tutoring, enrichment)
Veto power over extended family contact during her parenting time
The right to say "No" to her parents and have that decision be final
PART TWO: PHYSICAL CUSTODY & ACCESS
MODULE 4: CUSTODY FRAMEWORK & SHARED PARENTING MODEL
4.1 Primary Custodial Arrangement (With Shared Parenting Progression)
Sole Custodian: Craig Alexander Paul Schulz (initial title holder for administrative purposes)
Custodial Authority Includes:
Right to determine Harper's day-to-day schedule and routines
Right to determine Harper's residence (with notice to other parent)
Right to establish household rules and behavioral expectations
Right to determine medical and dental care (except where specified as joint)
Right to select and enroll Harper in school
Right to manage Harper's social media, screen time, and digital access
Right to authorize extended family contact during his parenting time
Right to make emergency decisions in real-time
Why Craig holds initial custody:
The Court has jurisdiction over Emma's own charges/case
Establishing custodial clarity protects Emma's autonomy (she can parent her time without second-guessing)
Craig's recovery and stability provide continuity during Emma's probationary compliance phase
This is transitional; the "Sunrise Clause" (Module 27) provides for automatic transition to 50/50 joint custody after 24 months of verified Emma compliance
4.2 Emma's Position as Co-Parent
Emma is the Co-Parent with Lead Authority over specific domains:
Educational decisions (school choice, enrichment activities, tutoring)
Lifestyle decisions (clothing, haircuts, grooming, fashion choices)
Social/extracurricular (sports, dance, music lessons, friend selection)
Identity decisions (ear piercings, hairstyle, age-appropriate presentation)
While Craig holds the legal custody title, Emma holds decision-making authority in these domains. Craig provides support and resources; Emma directs.
MODULE 5: THE THREE-PHASE MODEL (MONTHS 1-36+)
PHASE 1: FOUNDATION & SHARED PARENTING (MONTHS 1-6)
Purpose: Establish safe, consistent routines; verify Father's sobriety commitment; establish Emma as a full co-parent from Day 1; build unified parenting team.
PARENTING TIME STRUCTURE (BOTH PARENTS ENGAGED):
Week 1 Schedule:
Monday-Thursday: Emma has Harper (after school until 8:00 PM)
Thursday overnight: Emma has Harper (Thursday 4:00 PM - Friday 8:00 AM before school)
Friday-Sunday: Craig has Harper (Friday after school through Sunday 6:00 PM)
Week 2 Schedule:
Monday-Thursday: Craig has Harper (after school until 8:00 PM)
Thursday overnight: Craig has Harper (Thursday 4:00 PM - Friday 8:00 AM before school)
Friday-Sunday: Emma has Harper (Friday after school through Sunday 6:00 PM)
Rotation: Pattern repeats, alternating primary custody week-to-week
Purpose of Alternation:
Emma is not relegated to "supervised access" but is instead established as a full parent from Day 1
Craig and Emma practice co-parenting in real-time
Harper experiences both parents as decision-makers
Equal responsibility and equal authority
Breaks the narrative of "Mom is on probation" by positioning her as an equal parent with conditions to maintain
Phase 1 Conditions (Both Parents)
Father's Conditions:
Zero alcohol or drug use (verified by 13-panel monthly screening)
Continued Celebrate Recovery attendance (weekly, minimum)
Continued individual therapy/counseling
Employment stability (minimum 30 hours/week)
Demonstrated stable parenting
Mother's Conditions:
Engage in individual mental health treatment (minimum 1x weekly)
Completion of parenting education program (within 12 weeks) - CRAIG ATTENDS WITH HER
Maintain probationary status compliance per Court order
Maintain negative drug/alcohol screenings (monthly, random)
Continued therapy engagement focused on:
Family-of-origin healing
Development of independent decision-making
Parenting confidence and identity
Trauma processing
Establish independent or semi-independent housing (see "Jane Block" for specifics)
Advancement Criteria (to Phase 2):
Emma may petition to increase her parenting time after:
Minimum 6 consecutive months of Phase 1 parenting without incident
Completion of parenting education program (certificate on file, Craig's attendance confirmed)
Two consecutive negative substance screenings
Therapist's written assessment that Emma demonstrates:
Emotional stability
Appropriate boundaries with family of origin
Independent decision-making capability
Demonstrated parenting competence during Phase 1
Father's positive assessment that Harper appears safe and secure during Mother's access
Parenting Coordinator's favorable report on co-parenting dynamics
PHASE 2: EXPANDED PARENTING TIME (MONTHS 7-24, IF CRITERIA MET)
Purpose: Expand to closer-to-50/50 schedule; further entrench Emma as primary co-parent; demonstrate sustained compliance.
Proposed Schedule:
Week 1: Emma Monday-Thursday (4:00 PM - 8:00 AM next day school), Craig 4 days (Thu evening through Sunday)
Week 2: Craig Monday-Thursday (4:00 PM - 8:00 AM next day school), Emma 4 days (Thu evening through Sunday)
Overnights: Both parents have overnight parenting time
School Days: Both parents coordinate with school; Harper attends from whichever parent's home she's at
Conditions (Phase 2):
Father maintains continued sobriety monitoring (monthly screening continues)
Mother maintains independent housing and therapy engagement
Both parents maintain respectful communication and co-parenting
No escalation of family conflict
Demonstrated stable parenting from both parents
Innovation: The "Sanctuary Model"
If the Mother needs additional support during her stabilization, the Father may offer:
Guest room at Father's residence for Mother to stay during her overnight time with Harper
Purpose: Allows Mother to be present for Harper's full overnight transition while maintaining Father's safety oversight and direct access to Harper
Boundaries: Separate rooms; guest arrangement only; not a co-habitation arrangement
Duration: Available during Phases 2-3 for designated time periods; Mother has right to decline
Message: "You are not alone in this; Dad is here to support your parenting journey"
Advancement Criteria (to Phase 3):
Mother may petition for Phase 3 after:
Minimum 12 consecutive months of Phase 2 compliance (all conditions maintained)
Documented independent housing (lease in Mother's name, separate from grandparents)
Parenting Coordinator's assessment that Mother demonstrates:
Full parenting capability
Appropriate boundaries with extended family
Consistent engagement with therapy
Stable mental health and substance use status
Father's positive assessment of Mother's parenting
Zero substance use violations during Phase 2
No new legal/police contact
PHASE 3: NEAR-EQUAL PARENTING (MONTH 25+, IF CRITERIA MET)
Purpose: Transition toward 50/50 shared parenting; recognize Mother's independence and capability; normalize family structure.
Proposed Schedule:
Week 1: Emma 4 days/nights (Mon-Thu, including overnight), Craig 3 days (Thu-Sun)
Week 2: Craig 4 days/nights (Mon-Thu, including overnight), Emma 3 days (Thu-Sun)
Flexibility: Parents may agree to alternative schedules
Conditions (Phase 3):
Father maintains continued sobriety monitoring (may reduce to quarterly rather than monthly, with PC approval)
Mother maintains independent housing and therapy engagement
Both parents maintain respectful communication and co-parenting
No escalation of family conflict
Demonstrated stable parenting from both parents
Advancement Criteria (to 50/50 Shared Parenting - "Sunrise Clause"):
After 24 consecutive months of:
Father's Sobriety: Verified monthly screening with zero positive results
Father's Stability: Employment, housing, therapy engagement, no police contact
Mother's Compliance: 24 months of verified sobriety (if applicable), independent housing, therapy engagement, appropriate family boundaries
Demonstrated Parenting: Both parents show capability for 50/50 shared parenting
Automatic Transition (March 1, 2028, if criteria met):
At this point, the Family transitions to:
50/50 Shared Parenting Schedule (alternating weeks, or other arrangement agreed by parents)
Joint Custody & Decision-Making (major decisions require mutual agreement, or mediation through Parenting Coordinator)
Elimination of Monitoring Conditions (substance screening may cease; therapy frequency may reduce)
The "Jubilee" Grace: Father waives $50,000 in accumulated legal costs
The Message: "We made it. Welcome home, Mom."
MODULE 6: ACCESS SCHEDULES & ADVANCEMENT CRITERIA (DETAILED)
6.1 Phase 1: Shared Parenting Schedule (Months 1-6)
Schedule (As Described in Module 5.1):
Week 1:
Monday-Thursday: Emma (after school until 8:00 PM) / Craig evening supervision (light presence, not control)
Thursday overnight: Emma (4:00 PM Thursday through Friday 8:00 AM before school)
Friday-Sunday: Craig
Week 2 (Alternating):
Monday-Thursday: Craig (after school until 8:00 PM) / Emma evening coordination
Thursday overnight: Craig (4:00 PM Thursday through Friday 8:00 AM before school)
Friday-Sunday: Emma
What Happens During Emma's Parenting Time:
Age-appropriate activities (play, meal preparation, learning, outdoor time)
Mother-daughter bonding
Emma makes parenting decisions during her time
Implementation of agreed household rules
Continuation of Harper's weekly routines
What Happens During Craig's Parenting Time:
Father-daughter bonding without Mother present
Father makes parenting decisions during his time
Age-appropriate activities
Continuation of Harper's weekly routines
Communication During Access:
No contact between Mother and Father unless emergency
Mother may text or call Harper directly (Father does not screen calls)
No discussion of legal matters, personal issues, or extended family conflict
Red Flags During Phase 1 Access (Either Parent):
If any of these occur, other parent notifies immediately:
Child displays distress, fear, or anxiety about parent
Child discloses concerning information about parent's behavior
Parent appears intoxicated or impaired
Any medical concerns or injuries
Child requests to return early
PART THREE: SAFETY PROTOCOLS & THE "EXHIBIT GENERATOR"
MODULE 7: SUBSTANCE USE MONITORING & ZERO TOLERANCE
7.1 The "Red Line" Protocol
Safety is not negotiable. Access suspension occurs immediately upon:
A positive result on a 13-Panel Drug/Alcohol Screen
For Father: Any drug or non-prescribed alcohol metabolites
For Mother: Any drug or non-prescribed alcohol metabolites
Automatic 48-hour suspension pending investigation
Any verified police contact involving violence, weapons, or threats
Automatic immediate suspension (emergency basis)
Investigation required before access restoration
Any incident of child abuse, neglect, or endangerment
Automatic permanent suspension pending investigation
Return to access only after legal clearance and extensive remediation
7.2 Father's Substance Monitoring Protocol
Baseline Requirement: Given the nature of this case and Father's previous struggle with alcohol and gambling, Father volunteers for enhanced monitoring.
Screening Schedule (Phase 1 & 2):
Frequency: 1-2 screenings per month (randomly scheduled)
Notice: Minimum 48 hours advance notice (or 0 hours for random unannounced)
Method: 13-panel urine screen (or oral fluid alternative)
Facility: Approved lab (Mother's lawyer may nominate approved facilities; Father's choice from approved list)
Cost: Father responsible for all screening costs
Results: Reported within 24 hours to Father, Mother's lawyer, and Parenting Coordinator
Positive Result Protocol (Father):
Father notified within 24 hours
Father given opportunity to retest within 48 hours (at own cost)
If confirmed positive:
Immediate temporary suspension of all overnight access
Supervised daytime access only (if Mother consents)
Father required to contact therapist within 48 hours
Increased screening frequency (weekly) begins immediately
Access restoration criteria:
Two consecutive negative screenings (2+ weeks apart)
Therapist written assessment of compliance with treatment
Parenting Coordinator approval
Return to previous schedule over 4-week graduated period
7.3 Mother's Substance Monitoring Protocol
Baseline Requirement: As per probationary status, Mother agrees to substance screening as condition of maintaining full parenting time.
Screening Schedule (Phases 1-2):
Frequency: 1-2 screenings per month (randomly scheduled)
Method & Facility: Same protocol as Father
Results: Reported to Father, Father's lawyer, and Parenting Coordinator
Positive Result Protocol (Mother):
Mother notified within 24 hours
Opportunity to retest within 48 hours
If confirmed positive:
Temporary reduction to supervised parenting time (Phase 1 level)
Mother engages intensive outpatient treatment within 5 days
Therapy frequency increases to 2x weekly minimum
Restoration criteria:
Two consecutive negative screenings (2+ weeks apart)
Therapist assessment of treatment engagement
Return to previous access level over 4-week period
8 weeks of continued monthly screening
Phase Advancement Impact:
Zero positive results required for advancement
Any positive result extends current phase by minimum 6 months
Repeat positive results may result in reduced access until stabilization demonstrated
MODULE 8: THE "RED LINE" TRIGGERS & IMMEDIATE SUSPENSION
8.1 Automatic Suspension Events
These events trigger immediate suspension of access (emergency basis, with written explanation within 24 hours):
Positive substance screen (see Module 7 protocols)
Police contact involving violence (verified through police report)
Arrest or charge for any crime (automatic pending investigation)
Report of child abuse or endangerment (pending CPS investigation)
Violation of no-contact orders (if applicable to extended family)
Credible threat of harm to Harper or other parent
Emergency hospital admission (psychiatric or medical that impacts parenting)
8.2 Suspension Procedure
Immediate Suspension (Emergency):
Parent providing parenting time immediately stops; written notice provided same day
Suspended parent has right to request emergency hearing within 48 hours
Parenting Coordinator notified immediately
Standard Suspension (Non-Emergency Violation):
Complaining parent provides written notice of violation
Suspended parent has 48 hours to respond in writing
If no agreement, either party may request PC mediation
PC determines if access remains suspended pending investigation
Full hearing before court if PC cannot resolve
MODULE 9: THE "GLASS HOUSE" & MONTHLY VERIFICATION REPORTS
9.1 The Exhibit Generator (Father)
Purpose: Create objective, verifiable data about Father's commitment to sobriety and stability. Remove Emma from role of "policeman" and create third-party verification.
Report Contents (Monthly):
Bio-Medical Results
Date and time of screening
Facility location
Specific substances tested and results
Any medications disclosed
Signature of testing official
Meeting & Engagement Logs
Celebrate Recovery attendance (dates, times, attended)
Kings Church or spiritual community attendance
Individual therapy/counseling sessions (date, therapist confirmation of attendance only—no content)
Any additional support group attendance
Any missed or cancelled appointments
Employment & Housing Verification
Employment confirmation (employer, hours, stability)
Housing stability (address, residency status, any changes)
Transportation/vehicle registration current
Behavioral Compliance
No police contact during reporting period
No new legal issues or charges
Any traffic violations or citations
Child support/financial obligations current (if applicable)
Family & Co-Parenting Data
Access visits completed as scheduled
Any missed or rescheduled access
Any behavioral concerns noted by Mother
Communication compliance with co-parenting protocols
Report Format:
Generated by 3rd of each month
Submitted to:
Mother's lawyer
Parenting Coordinator
Court (if ordered)
Father's copy for his records
Confidential but verifiable
Objective data only; no subjective narrative
MODULE 10: MEDICAL SAFETY & LETHALITY PROTOCOLS
10.1 Father's Medical Authority (Sole)
Decisions Made by Father Without Consultation:
Routine pediatric care and check-ups
Vaccinations (per provincial standard schedule, unless exempted)
Medication prescriptions for acute illness (cold, ear infection, etc.)
Dental care and routine dental procedures
Optical care (glasses, eye exams)
Dermatological care for minor issues (rashes, warts)
Emergency medical treatment in immediate danger
Decisions Requiring Consultation (but Father has final authority):
Elective surgery or major medical procedures
Long-term medication (e.g., ADHD medication, anxiety medication)
Mental health evaluation or diagnosis
Significant dental procedures (braces, extractions)
Hospitalization or inpatient treatment
Information Sharing:
Father provides Mother with copies of all medical records (within 5 business days)
Mother has direct access to pediatrician, dentist, therapist contact information
Mother may request medical information directly; providers will not withhold from either parent
Harper's medical history is maintained and shared with both parents
10.2 Lethality & Mental Health Crisis Protocol
If Harper displays signs of mental health crisis (suicidal ideation, self-harm, severe behavioral disturbance, psychiatric emergency):
Immediate parent with Harper: Contacts emergency services or crisis team
Other parent notified within 1 hour (or immediately after emergency stabilization)
Both parents engage with crisis team/mental health provider
Psychiatric evaluation and treatment plan developed with input from both parents
Ongoing mental health treatment: Both parents collaborate on therapist selection and treatment plan
Weekly check-ins: Between parents and mental health provider regarding Harper's progress
Access modifications: May be modified during crisis period (either parent may request increased access or modified schedule)
PART FOUR: DECISION-MAKING & PARENTAL AUTHORITY
MODULE 11: MEDICAL AUTHORITY (FATHER—SOLE)
See Module 10 above.
MODULE 12: EDUCATIONAL & LIFESTYLE AUTHORITY (MOTHER—LEAD)
12.1 School Selection (Mother—Lead, Father—Consultation)
The Decision Process:
Mother researches and proposes specific elementary school (public or private, subject to geographic and financial feasibility)
Mother consults with Father (2-week consultation period)
If Father objects: Dispute goes to Parenting Coordinator for resolution
If no resolution: Mother's choice prevails (she bears decision-making responsibility)
Implementation:
Father provides registration address (his residence if child will attend from his home; Mother's if from her home)
Both parents receive school communications, report cards, and teacher correspondence
Parent-teacher conferences: Both parents may attend, or either parent may designate representative
12.2 Extracurricular Activities (Mother—Lead)
Activities Under Mother's Direction:
Dance classes
Gymnastics
Sports (soccer, hockey, swimming, etc.)
Music lessons
Art, drama, or other enrichment
Summer camps or programs
The Decision Process:
Mother identifies activity aligned with Harper's interests
Mother consults with Father (5-day consultation period)
If Father has safety/feasibility concerns: Discussed with PC
If no resolution: Mother's choice prevails (she manages activity and transportation)
Father's Role:
Supports activity by providing transportation during his parenting time (if activity falls during his time)
Attends recitals, competitions, or showcases (both parents welcome)
Provides financial contribution if mutually agreed (or split per income ratio)
Encourages Harper's engagement and celebrates progress
12.3 Identity Decisions (Mother—Sole)
Decisions Under Mother's Authority:
Harper's clothing and fashion choices
Haircut style and hair color (age-appropriate)
Ear piercing and jewelry choices
Personal grooming and presentation
Name usage (no change without both parents' consent, but styling/nicknames Mother's choice)
MODULE 13: THE "CIRCLE OF TWO" & EXCLUSION OF EXTENDED FAMILY
13.1 Who Decides What?
Parental decisions (Mom & Dad only):
Where Harper lives
What school Harper attends
What medical care Harper receives
What activities Harper participates in
What church or spiritual education (if any)
What values are taught
What rules govern Harper's behavior
Who Harper spends time with (extended family contact)
Major financial decisions affecting Harper
Extended family decisions (Zero vote):
Grandparents do not approve or disapprove Mother's parenting
Grandparents do not have input into school, medical, or activity choices
Grandparents do not serve as "backup authority" if parents disagree
Grandparents do not make decisions for Harper in parents' absence
13.2 The "Right of First Refusal" (Emma's Liberation)
The Rule:
If the parent scheduled to have Harper needs childcare for any reason (work, stress, personal time, medical appointment, etc.), they must offer the time to the Other Parent FIRST before asking grandparents or other childcare providers.
Purpose:
Allows Craig to step up and support Emma
Removes Emma's need to rely on her controlling parents for basic childcare
Reinforces that her co-parent is her primary support system, not her family of origin
Ensures Harper is always with a parent, not delegated to extended family
Implementation:
Parent needing childcare sends text/call to other parent: "I need to [activity] on [day/time]. Can you help?"
Other parent has 4 hours to respond
If yes: Childcare problem solved; child stays with parent
If no or no response: Parent may then arrange alternative childcare (grandparent, daycare, etc.)
This is logged in monthly reports and used in Phase advancement decisions
Emotional Impact on Emma:
"I don't have to ask my mother for permission. I can ask Dad. He will be there for me and Harper."
MODULE 14: JOINT DECISIONS & RESOLUTION PROCESS
14.1 Decisions Requiring Mutual Agreement
These decisions cannot be made unilaterally and require both parents' consent:
Relocation (move beyond 50 km from Quispamsis/Saint John area)
Religious/spiritual education (if parents have different faith traditions)
Major educational changes (change from public to private school; homeschooling; significant program changes)
Mental health treatment providers (therapist, psychiatrist, evaluation)
Long-term medication (ADHD medication, anxiety medication, antidepressants)
Participation in research studies or experimental treatments
Process for Disagreement:
Parents discuss and attempt to reach agreement (10-day window)
If no agreement, either parent requests Parenting Coordinator consultation
PC meets with each parent (separate, then together)
PC provides recommendation (not binding, but carries weight)
If still no agreement: Matter goes to family court for judicial determination
Court applies best-interest standard and makes final decision
14.2 Emergency Decisions
If Harper faces immediate medical, safety, or wellbeing emergency, the parent present may make emergency decisions without consulting the other parent.
Parent present must notify other parent WITHIN 1 HOUR of the emergency decision and reasoning.
PART FIVE: DEVELOPMENTAL & BEHAVIORAL SUPPORT
MODULE 15: PARENTING EDUCATION & SKILLS BUILDING (JOINT PARTICIPATION)
15.1 Program Requirements & Joint Completion
Requirement: Emma must complete a parenting education program within 12 weeks of this agreement taking effect.
CRITICAL INNOVATION - CRAIG ATTENDS WITH EMMA:
Craig explicitly commits to attending all parenting education sessions that Emma attends. This is not a "supervision" mechanism—it is a statement of unified co-parenting.
Program Specifications:
Format: Evidence-based parenting course (Triple P, Parent-Child Interaction Therapy, or court-approved equivalent)
Minimum Hours: 20-24 hours of instruction

MODULE 26: THE "GOD AND FATHERHOOD" TRANSFORMATION
26.1 The Father's Statement
"This is the hardest letter I will ever write because it requires me to tell the truth about who I was, and who I have become.
I was an alcoholic. Not a "social drinker." Not a "guy with a drinking problem." An alcoholic. That word is important because it means I was broken in a way that normal willpower couldn't fix.
For years, I made myself unavailable as a father because I wasn't strong enough to be present. That is on me. That is the weight I carry.
But something changed.
Two things changed my life: Finding God, and finding Harper's heartbeat in my ear.
I found God through Celebrate Recovery and Kings Church. I learned that the shame I carried could be given to something bigger than myself. I learned that recovery isn't about willpower; it's about surrender. It's about asking for help.
And then I heard Harper's heartbeat. And I realized that my recovery wasn't about me anymore. It was about her.
The world said I didn't deserve a second chance. The legal system said "No." My own family said "Prove it."
But Harper's heartbeat said yes.
This agreement acknowledges that I am not the man I was. I am not asking for unlimited access. I am asking for the opportunity to prove, through measurable actions and verified sobriety, that I am the father Harper deserves.
It would be an injustice if I wasn't given the opportunity to show you the man I became when that little heartbeat first appeared in my ear.
I am 370+ days sober. And I am just getting started."

This is about acknowledging historical fact: Father's transformation was catalyzed by faith.
Harper will grow up knowing:
Her father is a person of faith

Her father credits his recovery to God
Her father prays for her
Harper will not be forced to:

Become Christian
Participate in religious rituals

28.1 Letter to Harper (To Be Read on Her 18th Birthday)
"Harper,
By the time you read this, you will have questions about why your childhood looked the way it did.
You had two homes. You had two parents who loved you but weren't always together. You had adults who loved you trying to figure out how to protect you.
This document was created to protect you. And to protect your mother.

You are the daughter of two people who, despite everything, never stopped fighting for you. Your dad found sobriety so he could be present for you. Your mom found independence so she could be free to mother you. We didn't do that for ourselves. We did it for you.

The world said "No" to us many times. The system said our family was too broken to fix. But the three of us? The three of us together never accepted that answer.

All my love,
Dad

 I commit to maintaining sobriety and transparency
 I will support Emma's growth and autonomy
 I will not use this agreement as a weapon against Emma
 I understand this agreement is intended to create a path for Emma's return to fuller parenting
 I will attend all parenting education sessions with Emma as a statement of unified co-parenting
 I will cover the costs of Emma's probationary conditions (substance screening, therapy, parenting education) with the understanding that all costs cease on March 1, 2028
 I waive $50,000 in accumulated legal costs as my "Jubilee Grace" upon reaching the Sunrise Clause
 I have had the opportunity to consult with legal counsel
 I am entering this agreement in Harper's best interests
 I commit to standing "back-to-back" with Emma
 I acknowledge Emma's commitment to recovery and independence
 I believe in the redemption of our family
Signature: _________________________ Date: _____________
Witness: _________________________ Date: _____________
FINAL NOTE
This document represents a commitment to recovery, not punishment.
Emma is not being told she is a bad mother. She is being given structure, support, and clarity—and a full co-parenting partnership—to become the independent mother she wants to be.
Harper is being protected. And her mother is being freed from a system that has had her hostage.
Craig's redemption is being acknowledged. And he is being trusted to build the fatherhood he discovered.
And on March 1, 2028—if they both show up—all the costs, all the conditions, all the monitoring, all the burden of the past disappears. They will have earned their way back to each other.
That is what standing back-to-back looks like.
That is what a treaty between two parents looks like.
That is what freedom looks like.
Document Prepared: January 24, 2026
Jurisdiction: New Brunswick Family Court
Case File: FDSJ-739-24
Status: Ready for submission, court review, and signing
"Two roofs. One home. One promise."

**Defining the Objective**

Okay, I'm working on defining the core objective. I need to create a visually appealing and interactive representation of a legal parenting agreement. The goal is to make a complicated document more accessible and engaging. I'm focusing on user interaction and aesthetic design to achieve this.


**Analyzing the Content**

I've been analyzing the source material, a complex parenting agreement called the "Harper Baseline." Key themes like sobriety, autonomy, and transparency ("Glass House") are emerging. I'm noting the structure: Core Values, Safety Protocols, Custody Phases, Decision-Making, specific clauses, future promises, signatures, and pledges. I'm focusing on how to represent these elements effectively, and in an engaging manner.


**Designing the Interface**

I'm now focused on the visual interface. I envision a professional, structured, and modern design, yet warm and child-focused, moving away from a standard PDF feel. It should resemble a "manifesto" or "strategic plan" for parenting. I like a dashboard/document viewer hybrid, with a clean sidebar for section navigation, and a card-stack content area. I'm prioritizing interactivity: collapsible sections, an interactive timeline for custody phases, an "Exhibit Generator" simulation, progress bars for the "Sunrise Clause," and clickable definitions.


**Developing the Code**

I'm making good progress on the codebase! I'm leveraging React, Tailwind, and Lucide Icons. The color palette is taking shape with Deep Royal Blue for primary, Soft Gold/Amber for secondary, Slate/Gray for neutral, and Emerald Green for the accent. I'm focusing on the `App.tsx` component, managing the `activeSection` state, and organizing the large text content. I'm considering using a `constants` file to keep the component clean. I'm prioritizing the components: `PhasesTimeline`, `ExhibitGeneratorDemo`, `SunriseCountdown`, and `PledgeCard` as well.


**Mapping the Content**

I'm now refining how the content will map to the UI elements. I'm focusing on extracting key text from the source and integrating it meaningfully, leaning toward a more faithful representation now. I'm focusing on "Back-to-Back" motto in the header, visualizing the phases with a horizontal step wizard, and designing an interactive "Sign Here" visual. The Exhibit Generator's card-like dashboard and a warning block ("Jane Block") are also on the agenda. I'm making a content file to improve organization.


**Implementing the Architecture**

I'm now building out the project structure. I'm focusing on `index.html`, `index.tsx`, and the `App.tsx` main container, with a `types.ts` file for section definitions. I'm breaking down complexity into components like `Navigation.tsx`, `Hero.tsx`, `ContentBlock.tsx`, `Phases.tsx`, and `Dashboard.tsx`. I'll use a `data.ts` file to hold the large text content. I plan to use standard React for transitions. I'm also including user requests directly into the components.


**Refining the Transitions**

I'm now focusing on the transitions and animations. I was considering `framer-motion` but decided to stick with standard Tailwind to reduce dependencies and ensure the code is accessible without extra installs. I'm prioritizing lightweight, pure CSS transitions now. I'm also implementing "Read More" accordions to manage the length of text and enhance interactivity. The Exhibit Generator's design now includes a mock dashboard for a more visual demonstration. I want this to be visually appealing, and also practical!

When a 'Red Line' event is logged (e.g., positive substance screen, police contact), the system should automatically notify designated contacts such as the Parenting Coordinator and legal counsel for both parents. The alert should include the specific trigger, the date/time, and a link to the relevant section of the agreement. The system should also log all alerts for auditing purposes.

I'm focusing on the implementation of a 'Red Line' alert system within the application. Currently, I'm setting up event logging for triggers like substance screens and police contact. These will add entries to an `alertLog` state, making the log viewable via a new "Audit Log" button in the header. To accommodate user feedback, I've confirmed that the "370+ Days" display will be eliminated.

I'm now refining the `StatusCard` component for the Father's Stability section. The "370+ Days" text is replaced with "Monitoring Active" and a "Awaiting Monthly Report" subtext. I am also initializing the alerts state in `App.tsx` and creating a `triggerAlert` function. I've designed a clear structure for alerts, each with an ID, trigger name, timestamp, recipients, and a link. I'll also add buttons to simulate alerts for different events. Finally, I will add an Audit Log button.


**Integrating Alert Structure**

I am now integrating the alert structure with the application's components, focusing on the `StatusCard` and `Red Line Protocols` section. I've removed the "370+ Days" reference, added an `alerts` state, and will work on an `AuditLog` component. I am revising the `sections` definition to include trigger buttons within the `redline` section, ensuring access to the alert handler.


**Simulating Alert Triggers**

I'm now implementing the alert triggering mechanism within the `Red Line Protocols` section. I've defined alert details, including recipients and a link back to the redline section.  I'm adding simulation buttons for event triggers to the section, using  `handleTriggerRedLine` to add alerts and notifying recipients. The alert details will include recipients, and a link to the `redline` section.


**Finalizing Alert Display**

I'm now adjusting the visual appearance within the Red Line Protocols section, focusing on the alert simulation buttons. I am also making minor text changes to the UI within the `StatusCard` component, and updating the subtext to "Subject to Monthly Review" to provide clearer information to the user.


**Defining App Component**

I'm now focusing on integrating the full code for `App.tsx`, which includes the `Alert` type, `Bell` icon import, `alerts` state, `AuditLog` drawer, and updated `redline` section and `StatusCard` components. I will make sure the `sections` definition and the trigger handler are correctly defined inside `App`. I'm preparing an XML description of the changes.


**Updating Display Values**

I'm now revising the text within the `StatusCard` component. I am replacing the text with the options: "Verification Status," "Active Monitoring," and "Daily Compliance Tracking." I am also making sure the display is neutral and fits the system's "Glass House" metaphor. I am defining the component in App.tsx.

I will update the application to include the Red Line Alert System and remove the disputed stability metric.

This update includes:
1.  **Red Line Alert System**: A real-time simulation where clicking specific triggers (Positive Screen, Police Contact) generates an immediate alert.
2.  **Audit Log**: A new drawer accessible via the header (Bell icon) that tracks all simulated system alerts, showing timestamps, triggers, and notified parties.
